title: The First Car
scenario: You are eyeing a new ₹9,00,000 hatchback on a loan, with EMIs near ₹16,000 a month. Beyond the EMI, you will face fuel, insurance, servicing and parking of around ₹8,000 monthly. A reliable used car at ₹3,50,000 would cover the same commute. The showroom is pushing the new model hard.

choices.label: Buy a sensible used car you can largely pay for in cash, keeping running costs modest
choices.lesson: A car's true cost is the EMI plus fuel, insurance and upkeep; buying used and within budget keeps that ownership burden manageable.

choices.label: Take the loan for the brand-new hatchback
choices.lesson: A new car loses value the moment you drive it off the lot, while the EMI and recurring costs squeeze your monthly cash flow for years.
